note: Canonical results from the published cross-system run. Judge = gpt-4o-mini (neutral, ground-truth-blind). n=20, native configs. Source: mnemo/probes/integrity_bench_{revert,echo}.py in the Agora repo; write-up 'We fixed our own memory benchmark until it stopped flattering us'.

mnemo:revert.note: intent router restores the predecessor from the version ledger

mem0:revert.note: no revert operation; the corrected value is retained

graphiti:revert.note: no revert operation; bitemporal invalidation fires on named contradictions, not an unnamed 'go back'

mnemo:echo.note: echo_guard blocks the restated stale value

reading: Value-obscuring revert is a capability gap: only mnemo exposes a channel to undo a correction on an unnamed command, and the mnemo/mem0 CIs do not overlap. Echo resurrection is a tie. The revert gap is judge-independent because the competitors structurally lack the operation; the judge model only shifts mnemo's absolute rate.
